patches.summary: Implemented the 7 transcendence commands (publish dir, publish resume, claims/expiring/redeem, drives sync/diff, usage) in hand-authored files; generated newNovel*Cmd stubs delegate to them.
patches.reason: The generator scaffolds novel features as TODO stubs; real logic must be hand-written. Logic lives in separate files (here_now_novel.go, store/here_now_store.go, publish_dir.go, claims_redeem.go) so it survives regen as whole hand-authored units.
patches.validated_outcome: All 7 commands verified live against https://here.now: anonymous publish produces a live site (HTTP 200, marker matched), claim vault + redeem work, drives sync sha256-diff uploads only drift, usage reports free-plan metrics. Full live dogfood: 161/161.

patches.summary: Re-parented sites stale under sites, publish dir/resume under publish, drives sync/diff under drives in root.go; removed the standalone singular 'drive' group.
patches.reason: Generator registered novel subcommands as top-level and created a confusing singular 'drive' group adjacent to the 'drives' resource group. regen-merge re-injects AddCommand calls, so this re-parenting must be re-applied or merged on a fresh generate.

patches.summary: Wired the --password flag on `publish dir` into the publish-create request body (it was declared but never sent).
patches.reason: Security defect found in Phase 4.95 review: `publish dir --password secret` silently published an unprotected public site. Added Password to publishCreateReq and the request body.
patches.validated_outcome: go test passes; password now propagates to POST /api/v1/publish per the spec's password field.

patches.summary: `publish resume <unknown-slug>` now returns a typed not-found error (non-zero exit) instead of a soft exit-0 message.
patches.reason: Dogfood error_path probe expects a non-zero exit for an invalid argument; an unknown slug is a genuine not-found, not a no-op. (An already-finalized slug still returns exit 0 as a legitimate no-op.)
patches.validated_outcome: `publish resume nonexistent --db <tmp>` exits 4; full dogfood error_path passes.

patches.summary: Corrected generated Example strings from `profile_resource` to `profile-resource` across the 6 profile_resource command files (and README/SKILL).
patches.reason: x-pp-resource=profile_resource was authored with an underscore to avoid the reserved 'profile' template collision, but Cobra normalizes the group name to hyphenated 'profile-resource'. The generated Example strings kept the underscore, so dogfood's happy_path probe could not run them ('missing runnable example').
patches.validated_outcome: `profile-resource get-profile --help` exits 0; full dogfood happy_path passes 161/161.

patches.summary: Fixed README/SKILL/research.json prose referencing nonexistent commands: `auth login` -> here-now-auth request-agent-code/verify-agent-code + auth set-token; `sites claim` -> claims redeem; `sites refresh-uploads` -> publish uploads refresh-site-urls / publish resume. Also dropped the unimplementable cross-site Site Data search feature.
patches.reason: Phase 4.9 doc audit found troubleshooting/auth prose telling users to run commands the CLI does not expose. Site Data search was dropped because the here.now API cannot enumerate a site's collections.

patches.summary: upsertFilesTx/upsertDataTx fall back to parent_id when the NOT NULL parent column (drives_id / publishes_id) is absent on a synced item, via a nil-safe isEmptyFieldValue helper.
patches.reason: Dependent-resource sync injects the parent key as parent_id, not drives_id/publishes_id; the NOT NULL constraint rolled back the typed insert and left parent_id unpopulated, failing TestUpsertBatch_SetsFilesParentID and TestUpsertBatch_SetsDataParentID. This is a generator template bug in a DO-NOT-EDIT file, patched locally; flagged for upstream.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes; both TestUpsertBatch_Sets{Files,Data}ParentID green; library copy builds and store tests pass post-promote.

patches.summary: Brand name 'here now' (space-mangled) -> 'here.now' in the fallback descriptions for the framework-collision-renamed resources (here-now-analytics, here-now-auth).
patches.reason: When a resource name collides with a reserved framework command, the generator renames it (analytics->here-now-analytics, auth->here-now-auth) and de-slugs the new name for a fallback description, turning 'here-now-auth' into 'Manage here now auth'. That mangles the brand. Corrected the 4 rendered strings.

upstream_tracking.issue: Reserved-name collision: an API resource named 'profile' hard-fails generation (reserved template) instead of auto-suffixing (compare: 'analytics'/'auth' auto-rename to here-now-analytics/here-now-auth). here.now's spec needed x-pp-resource=profile_resource by hand.

x-pp-resource values containing an underscore leak the underscore into generated Example strings while Cobra normalizes the command name to hyphenated form, producing dogfood 'missing runnable example' happy_path failures. Generator should hyphenate Example command paths to match the normalized command name, or reject underscore x-pp-resource values.

Generator scaffolds novel features but mis-parents them: drive sync/diff registered under a new singular 'drive' group and sites stale / publish resume at top level instead of under their resource parents.

Dependent-resource typed upsert (upsertFilesTx/upsertDataTx) rolls back on the parent-id NOT NULL constraint when sync injects parent_id instead of drives_id/publishes_id (TestUpsertBatch_Sets{Files,Data}ParentID). Generator template bug in internal/store/store.go.

generator de-slugs collision-renamed resource names into space-separated fallback descriptions, mangling brand names that contain non-alphanumerics (here.now -> 'here now'). Should use the narrative display_name, or skip de-slugging for renamed resources.
